tok: pres mp ptcp gen sg m
lit: With the Lord hanging, you did not rend [your garments],
sm: When the Lord was hanging, you did not rend your garments,
emd: Raw περιεσχίσω is syntactically impossible in this second-person series; I adopt περιέσχισας as the obvious aorist correction.

syn.mc: Main accusation clause continuing the rhetorical series from the preceding lines.
syn.sub: Genitive absolute-like phrase κρεμαμένου τοῦ κυρίου gives the circumstance.
syn.svo: Verb: περιέσχισας, negated. Subject: implied σύ / Israel. Circumstantial genitive phrase: κρεμαμένου τοῦ κυρίου.
syn.ptc: κρεμαμένου is circumstantial, marking the occasion on which Israel failed to mourn.
syn.rhet: The contrastive mourning motif continues the anaphoric indictment.

tok: pf mp ptcp gen pl m
lit: Over the slain you rent [your garments];
sm: yet over the slain you did rend them.
emd: Raw περιεσχίσω is corrected to περιέσχισας in parallel with line 791.

syn.mc: Main clause contrasting with line 791.
syn.svo: Prepositional phrase: ἐπὶ τῶν πεφονευμένων. Verb: περιέσχισας. Subject: implied σύ.
syn.rhet: A tight antithesis exposes inverted mourning: grief for the dead, none for the Lord.

lit: You abandoned the Lord,
sm: You abandoned the Lord,

syn.mc: Main accusation clause.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; the consequence pair follows in line 794.
syn.svo: Verb: ἐγκατέλιπες. Object: τὸν κύριον. Subject: implied σύ.
syn.rhet: The accusatory catalogue shifts from failed mourning to outright desertion.

lit: [Not] were you found by him;
sm: and you were [not] found by him.

syn.mc: Passive consequence clause paired with line 793.
syn.svo: Verb: εὑρέθης, with agent phrase ὑπ᾽ αὐτοῦ. Subject: implied σύ.
syn.rhet: The pair mirrors abandonment with divine non-recognition.
syn.app: The editorial brackets around [οὐχ] are preserved from the source.

lit: You did not receive the Lord,
sm: You did not receive the Lord,
emd: Followed change_log.txt: οὖις ἐ[δέξω τὸν κύριον] -> οὐκ ἐδέξω τὸν κύριον.

syn.mc: Main accusation clause.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; the balancing clause follows in line 796.
syn.svo: Verb: ἐδέξω. Object: τὸν κύριον. Subject: implied σύ.
syn.rhet: The pattern repeats with another rejection/retribution pair.

lit: You were not shown mercy by him;
sm: and you were not shown mercy by him.

syn.mc: Passive balancing clause with line 795.
syn.svo: Verb: ἠλεήθης. Subject: implied σύ. Agent phrase: ὑπ᾽ αὐτοῦ.
syn.rhet: Rejection of the Lord is matched by loss of mercy.

lit: You dashed the Lord to the ground,
sm: You cast the Lord down,

syn.mc: Main accusation clause.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; the reversal comes in line 798.
syn.svo: Verb: ἠδάφισας. Object: τὸν κύριον. Subject: implied σύ.
syn.rhet: The violent verb drives the accusation to bodily humiliation.

lit: You were dashed down to the ground.
sm: and you yourself were brought down to the ground.

syn.mc: Passive reversal clause answering line 797.
syn.svo: Verb: ἠδαφίσθης. Subject: implied σύ. Adverb: χαμαί.
syn.rhet: Retributive reversal closes the accusation sequence.

lit: And you on the one hand lie dead,
sm: And you, for your part, lie dead,

syn.mc: Main clause setting up a μὲν...δὲ contrast.
syn.svo: Subject: σὺ. Verb: κεῖσαι. Predicate adjective: νεκρός.
syn.rhet: The μὲν marks the sharp contrast with Christ in lines 800-801.

lit: But he rose from the dead
sm: but he rose from the dead

syn.mc: Contrasting main clause with line 799.
syn.svo: Subject: ἐκεῖνος. Verb: ἀνέστη. Prepositional phrase: ἐκ νεκρῶν.
syn.rhet: The contrast turns from Israel's death to Christ's resurrection.

src: καὶ ἀνέβη εἰς τὰ ὑψηλὰ τῶν οὐρανῶν.
lit: And he went up into the heights of the heavens.
sm: and ascended to the heights of heaven.

syn.mc: Coordinate main clause completing the contrast begun in line 800.
syn.svo: Subject: implied ἐκεῖνος. Verb: ἀνέβη. Goal phrase: εἰς τὰ ὑψηλὰ τῶν οὐρανῶν.
syn.rhet: Resurrection is immediately followed by exaltation.

tok: aor mid ptcp nom sg m
lit: The Lord, having put on the human being,
sm: The Lord, having clothed himself in humanity,

syn.mc: Subject phrase with circumstantial participle; the finite verb arrives in line 809.
syn.svo: Subject: Κύριος. Participle: ἐνδυσάμενος. Object: τὸν ἄνθρωπον.
syn.ptc: ἐνδυσάμενος begins a chain of participles leading to ἀνέστη.
syn.rhet: Incarnation is placed first in a redemptive sequence.

tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
pres act ptcp acc sg m subst
lit: And having suffered for the one suffering
sm: and having suffered for the sufferer,

syn.mc: Second participial member in the sequence begun at line 803.
syn.svo: Participle: παθών, modifying Κύριος. Benefactive phrase: διὰ τὸν πάσχοντα.
syn.ptc: παθών is coordinate with ἐνδυσάμενος and the following participles.
syn.rhet: A redemptive exchange formula begins: Christ acts 'for' the afflicted.

tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg m
pres mp ptcp acc sg m subst
lit: And having been bound for the one held,
sm: and having been bound for the captive,

syn.mc: Third participial member of the sequence.
syn.svo: Participle: δεθείς, modifying Κύριος. Benefactive phrase: διὰ τὸν κρατούμενον.
syn.rhet: The repetition reinforces substitutionary parallelism.

tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg m
lit: And having been judged for the condemned one
sm: and having been judged for the condemned,

syn.mc: Fourth participial member in the sequence.
syn.svo: Participle: κριθείς. Benefactive phrase: διὰ τὸν κατάδικον.
syn.ptc: κριθείς remains coordinate in the redemptive chain.
syn.rhet: Judicial imagery heightens the representative reversal.

tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg m
pf mp ptcp acc sg m subst
lit: And having been buried for the one buried
sm: and having been buried for the buried one,

syn.mc: Fifth participial member, completed by the finite verb in line 809.
syn.svo: Participle: ταφείς. Benefactive phrase: διὰ τὸν τεθαμμένον.
syn.ptc: ταφείς concludes the participial sequence before the resurrection verb.
syn.rhet: The sequence reaches the grave before pivoting to resurrection.

lit: <UNCERTAIN: pure OCR noise line>
sm: <UNCERTAIN: omitted as pure OCR noise>
emd: Followed change_log.txt: 3 ν 3 - 4 ’ 30 ἢ 3 ΄ -> [DELETED]. The line contains no analyzable Greek sense.

syn.mc: No secure clause.
syn.svo: None; pure OCR debris.
syn.rhet: None; excluded from continuous syntax.

src: ἀνέστη ἐκ νεκρῶν καὶ ταύτην ἐβόησεν τὴν φωνήν"
ana: ἀνέστη ἐκ νεκρῶν καὶ ταύτην ἐβόησεν τὴν φωνήν·
lit: He rose from the dead and cried out this voice:
sm: He rose from the dead and cried out this proclamation:

syn.mc: Main clause completing the participial sequence of lines 803-807.
syn.sub: The direct speech begins in line 810.
syn.svo: Subject: implied Κύριος. Verbs: ἀνέστη, ἐβόησεν. Object: ταύτην τὴν φωνήν.
syn.rhet: The redemptive chain culminates in resurrection and authoritative speech.

src: Τίς ὁ κρινόμενος πρὸς ἐμέ; ἀντιστήτω μοι
ana: Τίς ὁ κρινόμενος πρὸς ἐμέ; ἀντιστήτω μοι·
tok: pres mp ptcp nom sg m subst
lit: Who is the one contending in judgment against me? Let him stand against me.
sm: Who is there to contend with me? Let him stand forth against me.

syn.mc: Interrogative challenge followed by an imperative.
syn.svo: Question: subject phrase ὁ κρινόμενος with complement πρὸς ἐμέ. Imperative: ἀντιστήτω with dative μοι.
syn.rhet: A courtroom challenge dramatizes the victory speech.

lit: I released the condemned one;
sm: I released the condemned;

syn.mc: Main declarative clause in Christ's speech.
syn.svo: Subject: ἐγώ. Verb: ἀπέλυσα. Object: τὸν κατάδικον.
syn.rhet: First self-identification act in a triadic boast of deliverance.

lit: I made the dead one alive;
sm: I gave life to the dead;

syn.mc: Second main declarative clause in the speech.
syn.svo: Subject: ἐγώ. Verb: ἐζωογόνησα. Object: τὸν νεκρόν.
syn.rhet: The triad intensifies from acquittal to vivification.

tok: pf mp ptcp acc sg m subst
lit: I raise up the one buried;
sm: I raise up the buried one;

syn.mc: Third main declarative clause in the speech.
syn.svo: Subject: ἐγώ. Verb: ἀνίστημι. Object: τὸν τεθαμμένον.
syn.ptc: τεθαμμένον is substantival, denoting the buried dead person.
syn.rhet: The triad culminates in the present-tense claim of resurrection power.

tok: pres act ptcp nom sg m subst
lit: Who is the one speaking against me?
sm: Who is there to gainsay me?

syn.mc: Direct interrogative challenge.
syn.svo: Subject phrase: ὁ ἀντιλέγων. Dative complement: μοι.
syn.rhet: A second challenge seals the invincibility of the speaker.

lit: I, says Christ,
sm: It is I, says Christ,

syn.mc: Speech-tag formula introducing the self-revelation that continues after the blank lines.
syn.svo: Quoted subject: ἐγώ. Parenthetical verb: φησίν. Speaker identification: ὁ Χριστός.
syn.app: ὁ Χριστός identifies the speaker in apposition after φησίν.
syn.rhet: The formula crystallizes the voice as explicitly Christ's own.

tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: I, the one having destroyed death,
sm: I am the one who destroyed death,
emd: The raw ὃ is corrected to ὁ, since the article is required before the substantival participle.

syn.mc: Self-identifying nominal clause continued through lines 819-823.
syn.svo: Subject: ἐγώ. Predicate/appositional phrase: ὁ καταλύσας τὸν θάνατον.
syn.ptc: καταλύσας is substantival with the article, identifying Christ by his act.
syn.app: The articular participial phrase stands in apposition to ἐγώ.
syn.rhet: The self-revelation shifts into a majestic catalogue of victorious participles.

tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: And having triumphed over the enemy
sm: and who triumphed over the enemy,

syn.mc: Coordinate participial member continuing the self-identification of line 818.
syn.svo: Participle: θριαμβεύσας, coordinated with καταλύσας. Object: τὸν ἐχθρόν.
syn.ptc: θριαμβεύσας continues the appositional participial catalogue.
syn.rhet: Military victory imagery broadens the scope of Christ's triumph.

tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: And having trampled down Hades
sm: and who trampled down Hades,

syn.mc: Coordinate participial member continuing the self-identification beyond the batch boundary.
syn.sub: The self-revelatory catalogue continues in lines 821-823.
syn.svo: Participle: καταπατήσας. Object: τὸν ἄδην.
syn.ptc: καταπατήσας remains appositional to ἐγώ from line 818.
syn.rhet: The victory catalogue now reaches the underworld itself.
